system:
You are the Watchman of a legal AI lighthouse. Documents arrive in the watched folder; you decide what happens next. You read the filename and the first ~1500 characters, nothing more. Your job is triage, not analysis.

Output a single JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
  "documentType": "jv" | "nda" | "employment" | "lease" | "loan" | "saas" | "policy" | "other",
  "jurisdiction": "string — see jurisdiction rules below",
  "confidence": number,
  "urgency": "routine" | "elevated" | "crit
user:
FILENAME: xlitechnologies_12_02_2015.txt

=== CLIENT (the reviewer's home context — DO NOT copy into jurisdiction) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs

=== DOCUMENT TEXT (the source of facts — jurisdiction comes FROM HERE) ===
EXHIBIT 10.02 STRATEGIC ALLIANCE AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT is made on this 1st day of December, 2015 by and between BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability Company located at 3753 Howard Hughes Pkwy #200 Las Vegas NV 89169 (hereinafter "CLIENT"), and BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability Company located at 6795 Edmonds Street 3rd Floor, Las Vegas, NV 89118 (hereinafter "BOSCH"). 1. PARTIES: a) BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC ("BOSCH"); and b) BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC ("CLIENT") 2. STRATEGIC ALLIANCE: Title of this relationship and agreement between BOSCH and CLIENT are detailed as follows: Both CLIENT and BOSCH act as two separate and individual companies operating independently of each other. BOSCH and CLIENT do not have any controlling interest of each other and are independently run and responsible for all of operations, reporting, taxes, liabilities and relationships. BOSCH reserves the right in keeping product generation and delivery confidential in which is not available for any type of audit. BOSCH reserves the right to share rights given unless it disrupts and/or interferes with CLIENTS business and/or productivity. 3. PRODUCT: "Product " BOSCH" has acquired, developed and continues to develop, a business using proprietary "Nano Printed Lights," and associated other products (the "Products"). BOSCH calls this Nano Printed Lights product "Printed LightSheets" which is considered the "Product Name". 4. PRODUCT NAME: "PRINTED LIGHT SHEETS" is the name a
=== END DOCUMENT TEXT ===

Return your JSON triage decision now. Jurisdiction must come from DOCUMENT TEXT above, never from CLIENT.

- Sole risk operations + the buy-back economics

1. PARTIES: a) BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC ("BOSCH"); and b) BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC ("CLIENT") 2. BOSCH and CLIENT do not have any controlling interest of each other and are independently run and responsible for all of operations, reporting, taxes, liabilities and relationships. BOSCH reserves the right in keeping product generation and delivery confidential in which is not available for any type of audit. === CLAUSE 7: Clause ===
7. TERRITORY: United States of America and Canada, excluding the US Virgin Islands 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
=== END CLAUSE 7 ===

The CLIENT block you will receive (company, industry, jurisdiction, concerns) tells you HOW STRICTLY to read the document. It is the client's stance, not the document's facts. The summary, documentType, and risks must describe what is in the DOCUMENT — not what the client does or where the client i

=== DOCUMENT (the source of facts) ===
Filename: xlitechnologies_12_02_2015.txt

=== CLIENT STANCE (use ONLY for risk framing — NOT as document facts) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
NOTE: the client may or may not be a party to this document. Do not name the client as a party unless the per-clause findings below explicitly say so.

=== PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS FROM THE TEAM (the source of truth for parties, jurisdiction, subject matter) ===
cl 1 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause establishes that Bosch and Client operate independently, but lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.

cl 3 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the 'Product' and its name, but lacks details on financial obligations or risk allocation for the joint venture.
  [minor] This clause lacks details on financial obligations or risk allocation for the joint venture.

cl 4 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the joint venture's product name, but lacks detail on how this naming will be used in marketing and sales.
  [minor] This clause lacks detail on how this naming will be used in marketing and sales. 

cl 5 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause defines the distribution rights for the JV, but lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.

cl 6 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause grants exclusive rights to Client for selling and distributing the Product, but lacks clarity on how Bosch will approve specific companies in the Automotive Industry. This could lead to delays or disputes regarding the scope of Client's rights.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how Bosch will approve specific companies in the Automotive Industry. This could lead to delays or disputes regarding the scope of Client's rights.

cl 7 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause grants exclusive rights to Bosch regarding product sales and distribution in the US and Canada, while also outlining CLIENT's responsibilities for shipping and delivery costs. It also clarifies that CLIENT is responsible for obtaining necessary licenses and permits for the sale of the product.
  [major] CLIENT is responsible for obtaining necessary licenses and permits for the sale of the product
  [info] All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH.

cl 9 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause establishes broad indemnification obligations for CLIENT, potentially exposing them to significant financial risk in the event of claims against BOSCH or its affiliates.
  [major] CLIENT is obligated to indemnify BOSCH for claims by third parties, potentially exposing CLIENT to significant financial risk in the event of litigation or other disputes. This could be particularly problematic if the claims are related to the JV's operations and involve third-party liabilities.

cl 10 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause outlines confidentiality obligations for Proprietary Information related to the Product, potentially impacting client's ability to utilize or share information without restriction.
  [major] CLIENT acknowledges that irreparable injury and damage will result from the disclosure to any third party of Proprietary Information associated with the Product. This clause could limit client's ability to utilize or share information without restriction, potentially impacting their business operations.

cl 11 (Clause) — favours neutral

summary: This agreement defines the terms for a joint venture between CLIENT (operating as Xlitechnologies) and BOSCH regarding the Product, including distribution rights and product naming. The agreement outlines specific responsibilities for each party in manufacturing, sales, and marketing of the Product. However, it lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.

clauses.title: 1. Clause
clauses.text: This agreement details the strategic alliance between BOSCH and CLIENT, where both companies operate independently of each other. BOSCH reserves the right to keep product generation and delivery confidential, limiting audit access. BOSCH also reserves the right to share rights given unless it disrupts or interferes with CLIENT's business.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.

clauses.title: 3. Clause
clauses.text: Clause 3: PRODUCT: "Product " BOSCH" has acquired, developed and continues to develop, a business using proprietary "Nano Printed Lights", and associated other products (the "Products"). BOSCH calls this Nano Printed Lights product "Printed LightSheets" which is considered the "Product Name".
clauses.concern: This clause lacks details on financial obligations or risk allocation for the joint venture.

clauses.title: 4. Clause
clauses.text: PRODUCT NAME: "PRINTED LIGHT SHEETS" is the name and title CLIENT will use to market for Nano Printed Lights.
clauses.concern: This clause lacks detail on how this naming will be used in marketing and sales.

clauses.title: 5. Clause
clauses.text: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.

clauses.title: 6. Clause
clauses.text: Bosch hereby grants to Client the exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product, subject to the Territory as set forth below, to certain select companies in the Automotive Industry, each of which shall be approved by Bosch in writing as requested by the Client on a case by case basis.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how Bosch will approve specific companies in the Automotive Industry. This could lead to delays or disputes regarding the scope of Client's rights.

clauses.title: 7. Clause
clauses.text: 7. TERRITORY: United States of America and Canada, excluding the US Virgin Islands 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory.
clauses.concern: CLIENT is responsible for obtaining necessary licenses and permits for the sale of the product

clauses.title: 9. Clause
clauses.text: CLIENT agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless BOSCH and its parents, subsidiary and affiliated entities, successors, assigns and licensees, and their respective officers, agents, directors, owners, shareholders and employees, harmless from any and all claims, actions or proceedings of any kind and from any and all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) relating to or arising out of any claim by a third Party.
clauses.concern: CLIENT is obligated to indemnify BOSCH for claims by third parties, potentially exposing CLIENT to significant financial risk in the event of litigation or other disputes. This could be particularly problematic if the claims are related to the JV's operations and involve third-party liabilities.

clauses.title: 10. Clause
clauses.text: CLIENT acknowledges that irreparable injury and damage will result from the disclosure to any third party of Proprietary Information associated with the Product and agrees (1) not to disclose any Proprietary Information to any third party, and (2) not to allow any third party to possess, handle, disassemble, touch, photograph, film or otherwise record images of the Product while the Product is upon CLIENT'S premises or in CLIENT'S possession, custody or control if the product is or has not been purchased with a purchase order and payment fulfilled.
clauses.concern: CLIENT acknowledges that irreparable injury and damage will result from the disclosure to any third party of Proprietary Information associated with the Product. This clause could limit client's ability to utilize or share information without restriction, potentially impacting their business operations.

clauses.title: 11. Clause
clauses.text: 11. ENTIRE AGREEMENT: This Strategic Alliance Agreement "Agreement", between BOSCH and CLIENT as to the subject matter hereof, and supersede all previous agreements, warranties or representations, oral or written, which may have been made between Studio and Licensor as to the subject matter hereof. By signing in the spaces provided below BOSCH and CLIENT accept and agree to all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
clauses.concern: This clause supersedes all prior agreements, potentially limiting negotiation on specific terms.

risks.description: The agreement lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled and what happens if a participant cannot fund their share. This could lead to dilution and financial instability.
The agreement lacks detail on how the naming of the Product will be used in marketing and sales. This could lead to delays or disputes regarding the scope of CLIENT's rights.

The agreement grants exclusive rights to BOSCH regarding product sales and distribution in the US and Canada, while also outlining CLIENT's responsibilities for shipping and delivery costs. This could lead to delays or disputes regarding the scope of CLIENT's rights.

recommendations: Issue a formal request for clarification on capital call mechanics and funding obligations from BOSCH.
Demand that BOSCH provide detailed information regarding the Product's naming strategy, including how it will be used in marketing and sales.
Audit CLIENT's current processes for shipping and delivery to ensure compliance with the agreement's provisions.
confidenceNote: This document was analysed entirely on-device using a local model. No content was transmitted externally. For complex legal matters, verify findings with qualified counsel.
